passages.title: Traditional Farming System in Africa
passages.source_html:
Traditional Farming System in Africa 


A 


By tradition land in Luapula is not owned by individuals, but as in many other parts of Africa is allocated by the headman or head woman of a village to people of either sex, according to need. Since land is generally prepared by hand, one ulupwa cannot take on a very large area; in this sense, the land has not been a limiting resource over large parts of the province. The situation has already changed near the main townships, and there has long been a scarcity of land for cultivation in the Valley. In these areas registered ownership patterns are becoming prevalent. 


B 


Most of the traditional cropping in Luapula, as in the Bemba area to the east, is based on citemene, a system whereby crops are grown on the ashes of tree branches. As a rule, entire trees are not felled but are pollarded so that they can regenerate. Branches are cut over an area of varying size early in the dry season and stacked to dry over a rough circle about a fifth to a tenth of the pollarded area. The wood is fired before the rains and in the first year planted with the African cereal finger millet (Eleusine coracane). 


C 


During the second season, and possibly for a few seasons more the area is planted to variously mixed combinations of annuals such as maize, pumpkins (Telfiria occidentalis) and other cucurbits, sweet potatoes, groundnuts, Phaseolus beans and various leafy vegetables, grown with a certain amount of rotation. The diverse sequence ends with vegetable cassava, which is often planted into the developing last-but-one crop as a relay. 


D 


Richards (1969) observed that the practice of citemene entails a definite division of labour between men and women. A man stakes out a plot in an unobtrusive manner since it is considered provocative towards one’s neighbours to mark boundaries in an explicit way. The dangerous work of felling branches is the men’s province and involves much pride. Branches are stacked by the women and fired by the men. Formerly women and men cooperated in the planting work, but the harvesting was always done by the women. At the beginning of the cycle little weeding is necessary, since the firing of the branches effectively destroys weeds. As the cycle progresses weeds increase and nutrients eventually become depleted to a point where further effort with annual crops is judged to be not worthwhile: at this point the cassava is planted, since it can produce a crop on nearly exhausted soil. Thereafter the plot is abandoned, and a new area pollarded for the next citemene cycle. 


E 


When the forest is not available – this is increasingly the case nowadays – various ridging systems (ibala) are built on small areas, to be planted with combinations of maize, beans, groundnuts and sweet potatoes, usually relayed with cassava. These plots are usually tended by women, and provide subsistence. Where their roots have year-round access to water tables mango, guava and oil-palm trees often grow around houses, forming a traditional agroforestry system. In season some of the fruit is sold by the roadside or in local markets. 


F 


The margins of dambos are sometimes planted to local varieties of rice during the rainy season, and areas adjacent to vegetables irrigated with water from the dambo during the dry season. The extent of cultivation is very limited, no doubt because the growing of crops under dambo conditions calls for a great deal of skill. Near towns, some of the vegetable produce is sold in local markets. 


G 


Fishing has long provided a much-needed protein supplement to the diet of Luapulans, as well as being the one substantial source of cash. Much fish has dried for sale to areas away from the main waterways. The Mweru and Bangweulu Lake Basins are the main areas of year-round fishing, but the Luapula River is also exploited during the latter part of the dry season. Several previously abundant and desirable species, such as the Luapula salmon or mpumbu (Labeo altivelis) and pale (Sarotherodon machochir) have all but disappeared from Lake Mweru, apparently due to mismanagement. 


H 


Fishing has always been a far more remunerative activity in Luapula that crop husbandry. A fisherman may earn more in a week than a bean or maize grower in a whole season. I sometimes heard claims that the relatively high earnings to be obtained from fishing induced an ‘easy come, easy go’ outlook among Luapulanmen. On the other hand, someone who secures good but erratic earnings may feel that their investment in economically productive activity is not worthwhile because Luapulans fail to cooperate well in such activities. Besides, a fisherman with spare cash will find little in the way of working equipment to spend his money on. Better spend one’s money in the bars and have a good time! 


I 


Only small numbers of cattle or oxen are kept in the province owing to the prevalence of the tsetse fly. For the few herds, the dambos provide subsistence grazing during the dry season. The absence of animal draft power greatly limits peoples’ ability to plough and cultivate land: a married couple can rarely manage to prepare by hand-hoeing. Most people keep freely roaming chickens and goats. These act as a reserve for bartering, but may also be occasionally slaughtered for ceremonies or for entertaining important visitors. These animals are not a regular part of most peoples’ diet. 


J 


Citemene has been an ingenious system for providing people with seasonal production of high-quality cereals and vegetables in regions of acid, heavily leached soils. Nutritionally, the most serious deficiency was that of protein. This could at times be alleviated when fish was available, provided that cultivators lived near the Valley and could find the means of bartering for dried fish. The citemene/fishing system was well adapted to the ecology of the miombo regions and sustainable for long periods, but only as long as the human population densities stayed at low levels. Although population densities are still much lower than in several countries of South-East Asia, neither the fisheries nor the forests and woodlands of Luapula are capable, with unmodified traditional practices, of supporting the people in a sustainable manner. Overall, people must learn to intensify and diversify their productive systems while yet ensuring that these systems will remain productive in the future when even more people will need food. Increasing overall production of food, though a vast challenge in itself, will not be enough, however. At the same time, storage and distribution systems must allow everyone accesses to at least a moderate share of the total.

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1-4


Complete the sentences below with words taken from the passage. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 1-4 on your answer sheet.


1

1 In Luapula land allocation is in accordance with .

正确答案： 1 need##Need
Question 1

原文答案句：By tradition land in Luapula is not owned by individuals, but as in many other parts of Africa is allocated by the headman or head woman of a village to people of either sex, according to need. 

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问的是在Luapula土地分配是按照什么进行的。

定位关键信息：利用题干中的"Luapula land allocation"定位到A段，找到"allocated...according to need"这一关键信息。

解题思路：原文明确指出土地分配是根据"need"（需求）进行的，因此答案为"need"。题干中的"Luapula land allocation"与原文中的"land in Luapula is...allocated"对应，"according to need"直接给出了答案。


2

2 The citemene system provides the land with where crops are planted.

正确答案： 2 the ashes##ashes##The Ashes##Ashes
Question 2

原文答案句：Most of the traditional cropping in Luapula, as in the Bemba area to the east, is based on citemene, a system whereby crops are grown on the ashes of tree branches.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是citemene系统为土地提供了什么，其中作物被种植在上面。

定位关键信息：利用题干中的关键词"citemene system"和"where crops are planted"定位到B段第一句，该句明确提到了citemene系统是基于在树枝的灰烬上种植作物的。

解题思路：根据原文答案句" crops are grown on the ashes of tree branches"，可以确定citemene系统为土地提供了灰烬，作物就是种植在这些灰烬上。因此，答案为ashes。


3

3 During the second season, the last planted crop is .

正确答案： 3 vegetable cassava##cassava##Vegetable Cassava##Cassava
Question 3

原文答案句：The diverse sequence ends with vegetable cassava, which is often planted into the developing last-but-one crop as a relay.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是在第二个季节中，最后种植的作物是什么。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"the second season"和"the last planted crop"定位到C段中描述第二个季节种植作物的句子。

解题思路：根据C段原文句子"The diverse sequence ends with vegetable cassava..."可知，各种作物的种植序列最后是以(蔬菜)木薯结束的，因此最后种植的作物是vegetable cassava。题目中的"the second season"与原文中的"During the second season"对应，"the last planted crop"对应原文中的"ends with"，由此确定答案为vegetable cassava。


4

4 Under suitable conditions, fruit trees are planted near .

正确答案： 4 houses##Houses
Question 4

原文答案句：Where their roots have year-round access to water tables mango, guava and oil-palm trees often grow around houses, forming a traditional agroforestry system.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目问在合适条件下，果树被种植在什么地方附近。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"fruit trees"和"planted near"定位到E段中关于果树种植的描述。

解题思路：原文E段提到"mango, guava and oil-palm trees"（芒果、番石榴和油棕树）这些果树在根部全年能接触到地下水的情况下，常常被种植在"houses"（房屋）周围，形成传统的农林系统。因此，果树被种植在房屋附近，答案为"houses"。


Questions 5-8


Classify the following items with the correct description. Write your answers in boxes 5-8 on your answer sheet.


A Fish 

B Oxen 

C Goats 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
5

5 be used in some unusual occasions, such as celebrations.
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：Most people keep freely roaming chickens and goats. These act as a reserve for bartering, but may also be occasionally slaughtered for ceremonies or for entertaining important visitors. 

对应段落：I段

审题：题目问哪种动物在特殊场合（如庆祝活动）被使用。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的"unusual occasions"和"celebrations"定位到I段中"occasionally slaughtered for ceremonies or for entertaining important visitors"这一信息。

解题思路：原文中明确提到"goats"（山羊）在特殊场合（如仪式和招待重要访客）会被宰杀，这与题干中"unusual occasions, such as celebrations"完全对应。其他选项如"fish"和"oxen"在文中未被提及用于此类特殊场合。因此答案为C。
6

6 cannot thrive for being affected by the pests.
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Only small numbers of cattle or oxen are kept in the province owing to the prevalence of the tsetse fly. 

对应段落：I段

审题：题目问的是哪种动物因为受到害虫影响而无法生长。

定位关键信息：使用 "cannot thrive", "affected by the pests" 定位到文中描述某种牲畜因虫害而数量稀少的句子。

解题思路：在段落I中，原文明确指出“由于采采蝇的盛行，该省只饲养了少量牛或 oxen”。“由于采采蝇的盛行”(owing to the prevalence of the tsetse fly) 直接对应了“受害虫影响”，而“只饲养了少量”(only small numbers...are kept) 则对应了“无法大量繁殖”。因此，符合此描述的动物是B (Oxen)。
7

7 be the largest part of creating profit. 
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：Fishing has always been a far more remunerative activity in Luapula that crop husbandry. A fisherman may earn more in a week than a bean or maize grower in a whole season.

对应段落：H段

审题：题目问的是哪种动物创造的利润最大。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"largest part of creating profit"可以定位到H段中关于捕鱼和农作物种植的比较。

解题思路：根据原文H段的句子" Fishing has always been a far more remunerative activity in Luapula that crop husbandry. A fisherman may earn more in a week than a bean or maize grower in a whole season."可以得出结论，捕鱼一直是比农作物种植更赚钱的活动，渔民一周的收入可能比种植豆类或玉米的农民整个季节的收入还要多。因此，选择A。
8

8 be sold beyond the local area.
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：Much fish has dried for sale to areas away from the main waterways.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问的是哪种动物会被销往外地。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"beyond the local area"，可以在文章中寻找描述动物销售范围的句子。

解题思路：在G段中，原文提到"Much fish has dried for sale to areas away from the main waterways"（大量鱼被晒干用于销售到主水道以外的地区）。这句话明确指出鱼被销售到当地以外的地区，与题目要求一致。因此，正确答案是G段。

Questions 9-12


Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in the passage? In boxes 9-12 on your answer sheet, write TURE if the statement agrees with the information, FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this.


9

People rarely use animals to cultivate the land.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：For the few herds, the dambos provide subsistence grazing during the dry season. The absence of animal draft power greatly limits peoples’ ability to plough and cultivate land: a married couple can rarely manage to prepare by hand-hoeing.  

对应段落：I段  

审题：题目问“人们很少用动物来耕种土地”，该说法是否正确。  

定位关键信息：题目中的“animals to cultivate the land”对应文章中“animal draft power”和“hand-hoeing”等描述耕作方式的词汇。  

解题思路：I段明确提到“畜力的缺乏极大地限制了人们犁地和耕种土地的能力”，并指出“一对夫妇几乎无法靠手工锄地来完成准备工作”，说明人们很少使用动物进行耕作，而是依赖人力。因此，题目与原文信息一致，答案为TRUE。


10

When it is a busy time, children usually took part in the labor force.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：原文中没有直接提到儿童参与劳动力的句子。

对应段落：全文未提及。

审题：题目问的是“当农忙时，儿童也参与劳动”这一说法是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是“children”和“labor force”，需要寻找文章中关于儿童参与劳动的描述。

解题思路：文章中没有提到儿童在农忙时参与劳动力的任何信息。文章主要描述了传统农业系统中的耕作方式、劳动分工（如男女分工）、渔业和畜牧业等情况，但未涉及儿童参与劳动的具体内容。因此，无法确定题目中的说法是否与文章一致。



11

The local residents eat goats on a regular time.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：These act as a reserve for bartering, but may also be occasionally slaughtered for ceremonies or for entertaining important visitors. These animals are not a regular part of most peoples’ diet.

对应段落：I段

审题：题目问“当地居民每隔一段时间就会吃山羊”是否与文章作者的观点一致。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的“goats”和“regular time”定位到I段中关于山羊用途的描述。

解题思路：原文明确提到山羊“not a regular part of most peoples’ diet”，即这些动物通常不在大多数人的日常饮食中，而题目却说“regular time”（定期），两者意思相反，因此题目与原文信息矛盾，应选择FALSE。


12

Though citemene has been a sophisticated system, it could not provide enough protein.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：Citemene has been an ingenious system for providing people with seasonal production of high-quality cereals and vegetables in regions of acid, heavily leached soils. Nutritionally, the most serious deficiency was that of protein. 

对应段落：J段

审题：题目问的是“尽管citemene是一个复杂的系统，但它无法提供足够的蛋白质”，是否与文章作者的观点一致。

定位关键信息：根据题干中的关键词"citemene"和"protein"，可以在文章中定位到J段。该段明确提到了营养方面最严重的缺陷是蛋白质的缺乏。

解题思路：段落J中，作者首先肯定了citemene系统，称其为“一个巧妙的系统”(an ingenious system)，这与“复杂的系统”相符。紧接着，作者明确指出该系统在营养方面“最严重的缺陷是蛋白质不足”(the most serious deficiency was that of protein)。这直接证实了题目的第二个核心信息。因此，该陈述与作者在文中的信息完全一致，答案为TRUE。

Question 13


Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D. Write the correct letter in the box 13 on your answer sheet.


13

What is the writer’s opinion about the traditional ways of practices?

正确答案： B. They are not capable of providing adequate support to the population.

原文答案句：Although population densities are still much lower than in several countries of South-East Asia, neither the fisheries nor the forests and woodlands of Luapula are capable, with unmodified traditional practices, of supporting the people in a sustainable manner.

对应段落：J段

审题：题目问的是作者对传统农耕方式的看法是什么。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"traditional ways of practices"，可以在文章中定位到J段，该段提到了传统农耕方式（traditional practices）。

解题思路：根据J段中的原文答案句可知，尽管卢阿普拉地区人口密度仍然比东南亚的几个国家低，但如果不改进传统农耕方式，卢阿普拉的渔业、森林和林地都无法可持续地养活人们。因此，作者认为传统实践方式无法为人口提供充足的支持，这与选项B的表述一致。
